channel.title: dotnet/orleans Issues
channel.description: Issues from https://github.com/dotnet/orleans, generated by https://github.com/meziantou/IssuesToRss/

item.title: Issue: Client connection tracking during rolling deploys - @jsteinich
item.description:
I've been troubleshooting some issues during rolling silo deploys. Specifically, clients will often fire the ConnectionToClusterLost event despite the new silo(s) being available. I'm trying to determine a point at which the client(s) know about the new silo(s) at which we can safely shutdown the old silo(s).

I was thinking that waiting ~1 minute after the new silo is up would be enough (since gateway configuration is updated every minute on the client); however, I observed that even though the client knew about the new gateway (confirmed by refresh gateway logging), it still triggered the connection lost when the old server shutdown ~20 seconds later. Is there some other later point or some proactive work that could be done to ensure that shutdown is safe?

This mostly matters because we are shutting down the client on connection loss to make sure any client side streams are recreated cleanly.

Setup:

  • Separate clients and silos running in AWS ECS Fargate tasks
  • DynamoDB membership

item.title: Issue: CodeGenerator.MSBuild v3.4.3 does not work with records - @wdspider
item.description:
Using this basic grain:

    public sealed record LocationInfo
    {
        public static LocationInfo Default { get; } = new LocationInfo { Id = -1, Name = "Unknown Location" };

        public long Id { get; init; }
        public string Name { get; init; } = "";
    }

    public interface ILocationGrain : IGrainWithIntegerKey
    {
        public Task<LocationInfo> GetLocationInfoAsync();
        public Task SetInfoAsync(LocationInfo locationInfo);
    }

    public sealed class LocationGrain : Grain, ILocationGrain
    {
        #region Internal State
        private LocationInfo locationInfo = LocationInfo.Default;
        #endregion

        #region ILocationGrain Implementation
        Task<LocationInfo> ILocationGrain.GetLocationInfoAsync() => Task.FromResult(locationInfo);

        Task ILocationGrain.SetInfoAsync(LocationInfo info)
        {
            locationInfo = info;
            return Task.CompletedTask;
        }
        #endregion
    }

The code gen fails with these errors:

1>Orleans.CodeGenerator - command-line = SourceToSource C:\Repos\src\server\Abstractions\obj\Debug\net6.0\Abstractions.orleans.g.args.txt
1>EXEC : warning : attempted to get TypeSyntax for unknown (error) type, "LocationInfo". Possible reason: None. Possible candidates:
1>EXEC : warning : attempted to get TypeSyntax for unknown (error) type, "LocationInfo". Possible reason: None. Possible candidates:
1>EXEC : warning : attempted to get TypeSyntax for unknown (error) type, "LocationInfo". Possible reason: None. Possible candidates:
1>EXEC : warning : attempted to get TypeSyntax for unknown (error) type, "LocationInfo". Possible reason: None. Possible candidates:
1>C:\Repos\src\server\Abstractions\obj\Debug\net6.0\Abstractions.orleans.g.cs(102,45,102,57): error CS0246: The type or namespace name 'LocationInfo' could not be found (are you missing a using directive or an assembly reference?)
1>C:\Repos\src\server\Abstractions\obj\Debug\net6.0\Abstractions.orleans.g.cs(102,118,102,138): error CS0539: 'OrleansCodeGenLocationGrainReference.GetLocationInfoAsync()' in explicit interface declaration is not found among members of the interface that can be implemented
1>C:\Repos\src\server\Abstractions\obj\Debug\net6.0\Abstractions.orleans.g.cs(107,117,107,129): error CS0246: The type or namespace name 'LocationInfo' could not be found (are you missing a using directive or an assembly reference?)
1>C:\Repos\src\server\Abstractions\obj\Debug\net6.0\Abstractions.orleans.g.cs(107,104,107,116): error CS0539: 'OrleansCodeGenLocationGrainReference.SetInfoAsync(LocationInfo)' in explicit interface declaration is not found among members of the interface that can be implemented
1>C:\Repos\src\server\Abstractions\obj\Debug\net6.0\Abstractions.orleans.g.cs(57,99,57,157): error CS0535: 'OrleansCodeGenLocationGrainReference' does not implement interface member 'ILocationGrain.GetLocationInfoAsync()'
1>C:\Repos\src\server\Abstractions\obj\Debug\net6.0\Abstractions.orleans.g.cs(57,99,57,157): error CS0535: 'OrleansCodeGenLocationGrainReference' does not implement interface member 'ILocationGrain.SetInfoAsync(LocationInfo)'
1>Done building project "Abstractions.csproj" -- FAILED.

The above errors go away if I change LocationInfo to a class rather than a record. In new projects, I prefer for my POCO objects to be records instead of classes so that they can take advantage of the build in immutablity and value equals. Please fix.

item.title: Issue: Restart Silo in the cluster, how speed up the cluter recovery process? - @sunliusi
item.description:
I run silo in k8s, just restart deployment a few times, and reported the error:

Unhandled exception. Orleans.Runtime.MembershipService.OrleansClusterConnectivityCheckFailedException: Failed to get ping responses from 3 of 3 active silos. Newly joining silos validate connectivity with all active silos that have recently updated their 'I Am Alive' value before joining the cluster. Successfully contacted: []. Failed to get response from: [S10.42.7.47:11111:364100355, S10.42.5.212:11111:364100339, S10.42.9.83:11111:364100370] at Orleans.Runtime.MembershipService.MembershipAgent.ValidateInitialConnectivity() at Orleans.Runtime.MembershipService.MembershipAgent.BecomeActive() at Orleans.Runtime.MembershipService.MembershipAgent.<>c__DisplayClass26_0.<<Orleans-ILifecycleParticipant-Participate>g__OnBecomeActiveStart|6>d.MoveNext() --- End of stack trace from previous location --- at Orleans.Runtime.SiloLifecycleSubject.MonitoredObserver.OnStart(CancellationToken ct) at Orleans.LifecycleSubject.OnStart(CancellationToken ct) at Orleans.Runtime.Scheduler.AsyncClosureWorkItem.Execute() at Orleans.Runtime.Silo.StartAsync(CancellationToken cancellationToken) at Orleans.Hosting.SiloHost.StartAsync(CancellationToken cancellationToken) at Orleans.Hosting.SiloHostedService.StartAsync(CancellationToken cancellationToken) at Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting.Internal.Host.StartAsync(CancellationToken cancellationToken) at Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting.HostingAbstractionsHostExtensions.RunAsync(IHost host, CancellationToken token) at Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting.HostingAbstractionsHostExtensions.RunAsync(IHost host, CancellationToken token)

When this error occurs, there is a slow recovery process during which the system is unavailable. How can I speed up the recovery process?

[silo_63ebdfaa-8]: Azure table access write error InsertTableEntryConditionally to table OrleansSiloInstances entry OrleansSilo [ Deployment=signallingcluster LocalEndpoint=172.20.128.1 LocalPort=11111 Generation=363341917 Host=MATH Status=Joining ProxyPort=30000 RoleName=Platform.Signalling.Orleans.Silo SiloName=Silo_7ddc8 UpgradeZone=0 FaultZone=0 StartTime=2021-07-07 08:18:37.846 GMT IAmAliveTime=2021-07-07 08:22:21.306 GMT] [silo_63ebdfaa-8]: System.ArgumentException: Replace requires an ETag (which may be the '*' wildcard).

Orleans successfully writes a single row then fails with the above error. Not sure if this is an issue with Orleans or Azurite. Might be that the Azurite API deviates from Azure?

item.title: Issue: Reducing time between checkpoints with ConfigureCacheEviction - @cjenp
item.description:
Hi,

our solution is using an event hub stream provider to receive data from our IoT devices (Azure IoT Hub). We noticed that event thought our checkpointer persist interval is set to a few seconds, the checkpoint does not get updated. After some searching I have found that the checkpoint is updated when events are purged from the message cache (#6581).

Our problem is as follows: we are receiving messages from devices via IoT Hubs build-in event hub (at most 4 per minute). If we shutdown and restart our silo host, the messages from the stream get replayed from the latest checkpoint (if I understand correctly this can be up to 30min in the past with the default settings). Since our grains are idempotent, each replayed message that was already processed by the grain throws an exception. The stream then also retries the duplicate messages a few times. This causes our grains to fall behind, and they need a few minutes after silo restart to start processing the messages received in real-time.

To solve this we would like to adjust the cache eviction options to happen at least once per minute. Would this cause any issues with the stream or affect its performance? Our thinking is, that since once the messages from the stream are successfully processed we don’t need them anymore, so there is no point in keeping them in the cache for that long.

Also, is it possible to somehow configure or disable retrying of failed messaged received from the stream?

item.title: Issue: TlsClientConnectionMiddleware does not have a certificate selector - @andyatwork
item.description:
There is no way to reload a certificate through a selector on a new client connection. Ideally, the client would TlsOptions.LocalServerCertificateSelector over TlsOptions.LocalCertificate when specified, just like in TlsServerConnectionMiddleware.

Doing so would allow certificate rotations, say through Azure KeyVault (and VM extension) to occur seamlessly and without a process restart.

item.title: Issue: How to Store Transaction State In Regular Storage If Transaction Is Successful - @nkosi23
item.description:
What would be the correct pattern to save a transaction state in regular storage (grain storage, custom storage...) immediately after a successful transaction. I have a complex grain having custom storage logic. I already have a place to store the properties/state involved in the transaction and I do not want to spread the domain model across several data stores.

I do not mind using ITransactionState as some sort of transient data store enabling Orleans to manage the distributed transaction, however looking at the documentation, it looks like Orleans is forcing us to store the domain data involved in a transaction as TransactionalState, which looks weird seems this feature is advertised as a way to allow us to run distributed ACID transactions across heterogeneous data stores (even across systems).

As a result, I have a hard time believing that having to fit all reads and writes in synchronous PerformRead/PerformUpdate methods is the whole story, I have to be missing something. Unfortunately the documentation does not address this major use case. If PerformRead/PerformUpdate accepted two async delegates allowing us to define async remote calls to save and rollback state, this would have made more sense to me. This is the kind of API I am after.

I am looking for the API/hook that would allow me to save data wherever I want at the end of a transaction. Saving data in custom stores at the end of the method marked as TransactionOption.Create seems risky since if I need to commit the data to 3 different data stores (say A, B and C), and the last commit to the data store C fails, having to write this kind of rollback code looks a lot like defeating the whole purpose for which this feature was created in the first place.

item.title: Issue: Orleans CodeGenerator (MSBuilld) fails when DTOs are placed in separate namespace - @syberside
item.description:
Assuming following folder/namespace structure

Grains.Interfaces.dll - NS Grains.Interfaces
`-Models
  `-MyModel.cs - NS Grains.Interfaces.Models
`-IMyGrain.cs - NS Grains.Interfaces

Generated code will be placed in NS Grains.Interfaces, but MyModel references will not contains NS. In the same time Interface reference will always use full NS in generated code.

item.title: Issue: Question: Migrating reminders from DynamoDB to Azure Table Storage - @benthepoet
item.description:
I'm currently working on migrating our Orleans backend from AWS over to Azure with minimal downtime.

For grain storage we took the approach of implementing a custom provider that checks if the grain state is present in Azure Blob Storage and if not then it pulls it from DynamoDB and writes the state into Azure. This allows us to live migrate grain states over as they're activated.

Now we're looking at how to migrate our reminders from DynamoDB to Azure Table Storage. Is there a similar strategy to how we're migrating grain storage available? Can we do this live or does it require taking all the silos down? We have less than 100 reminders in production, so it's a rather small set of data.

The two cluster nodes logs are attached. I hope you will find the way to help me. Thank you. noreO.csv nodeI.csv

item.title: Issue: Host and Client communication in Kubernetes - @alishadas17
item.description:
Hi, Need help in the deployment of the host and client in Kubernetes. I am using Orleans.Hosting in my Host project, to extend the UseKubernetesHosting() method. Here is the code snippet of program.cs of my host:

await Host.CreateDefaultBuilder(args) .UseOrleans((ctx, siloBuilder) => { siloBuilder.UseKubernetesHosting(); siloBuilder.AddAdoNetGrainStorageAsDefault(options => { options.Invariant = "Oracle.DataAccess.Client"; options.ConnectionString = "My Connection String"; }); siloBuilder.UseAdoNetClustering(options => { options.Invariant = "Oracle.DataAccess.Client"; options.ConnectionString = "My Connection String"; }); siloBuilder.Configure(options => { options.SiloPort = 11111; options.GatewayPort = 30000; options.AdvertisedIPAddress = IPAddress.Loopback; }); siloBuilder.Configure(options => { options.ClusterId = "dev"; options.ServiceId = "DemoApp"; }); siloBuilder.ConfigureApplicationParts(parts => parts.AddApplicationPart(typeof(TestStateGrain).Assembly).WithReferences()); }) .RunConsoleAsync();

Looks like this is running in Kubernetes: info: Orleans.Networking[0] Connection [Local: 127.0.0.1:11111, Remote: 127.0.0.1:56976, ConnectionId: 0HM9QSISSMI69] terminated info: Orleans.Runtime.MembershipService.MembershipTableManager[100645] ProcessTableUpdate (called from Refresh) membership table: 2 silos, 1 are Active, 1 are Dead, Version=<2903, 2903>. All silos: [SiloAddress=S127.0.0.1:11111:362656822 SiloName=orleanhost-pks-5fc87c75b4-47gjf Status=Active, SiloAddress=S127.0.0.1:11111:362656828 SiloName=Silo_8e8ad Status=Dead] info: Orleans.Runtime.Catalog[100524]

However, I'm seeing it's using Local: 127.0.0.1:11111. My 1st query is about this configuration. What should be put in options.AdvertisedIPAddress? (Can't ignore AdvertisedIPAddress as it giving error: System.ArgumentNullException: 'Value cannot be null. (Parameter 'address')')

Now, I need to connect my client to this host. Here is my code snippet of Client Program.cs: var clientBuilder = new ClientBuilder() .UseAdoNetClustering(options => { options.Invariant = "Oracle.DataAccess.Client"; options.ConnectionString = "My Connection string"; }) //Clustering information .Configure(options => { options.ClusterId = "dev"; options.ServiceId = "DemoApp"; }); client = clientBuilder.Build();
client.Connect(async ex => { Console.WriteLine($"\n\nCould not connect to Orleans cluster: {ex}\n\n"); await Task.Delay(TimeSpan.FromMilliseconds(300)).ConfigureAwait(false); return true; }).GetAwaiter().GetResult(); //Console.WriteLine("Client successfully connected to silo host \n"); return client;

What changes I need to do here in client code to get it connected to the host in Kubernetes? Now I am getting the below error: Could not connect to Orleans cluster: Orleans.Runtime.Messaging.ConnectionFailedException: Unable to connect to S127.0.0.1:30000:0, will retry after 36.6679ms at Orleans.Runtime.Messaging.ConnectionManager.ConnectionEntry.ThrowIfRecentConnectionFailure() at Orleans.Runtime.Messaging.ConnectionManager.GetConnectionAsync(SiloAddress endpoint

item.title: Issue: TransactionAbortedException and sql server connection does not restore - @luyangdev
item.description:
Hello, I got TransactionAbortedException after restarting sql server. SqlServer connection does not restore.

Orleans.Transactions.OrleansTransactionAbortedException: Transaction 4357009a-e08b-4866-bce4-11f8a95ed22c Aborted because of an unhandled exception in a grain method call. See InnerException for details.
 ---> Orleans.Runtime.OrleansException: Error from storage provider AdoNetGrainStorage.TestGrains.TaskGrain,TestGrains-TaskGrain during ReadState for grain Type=TestGrains.TaskGrain,TestGrains-TaskGrain Pk=*grn/TestGrains.TaskGrain/0+key-0xA0398413 Id=GrainReference:*grn/TestGrains.TaskGrain/0+key Error=

Exc level 0: System.Data.SqlClient.SqlException: A network-related or instance-specific error occurred while establishing a connection to SQL Server. The server was not found or was not accessible. Verify that the instance name is correct and that SQL Server is configured to allow remote connections. (provider: Named Pipes Provider, error: 40 - Could not open a connection to SQL Server)

Reproduce: github_repo In the Step 3 below, I expect sql server connection to be restored and no exception to be thrown, however, the above exception still happened.

using var client = BuildClientSqlServer();

await client.Connect();

Console.WriteLine("\n== Step 1. Should OK ==");
Console.WriteLine("Make sure sql server is running now, and press any key to continue");
Console.ReadKey();
var result = await client.GetGrain<ITaskGrain>("key").Execute();
Console.WriteLine(result.Count);

Console.WriteLine("\n== Step 2. Should Exception ==");
Console.WriteLine("Stop sql server now, and press any key to continue");
Console.ReadKey();

try
{
    result = await client.GetGrain<ITaskGrain>("key").Execute();
    Console.WriteLine(result.Count);
}
catch (Exception ex)
{
    Console.WriteLine(ex.ToString());
}

Console.WriteLine("\n== Step 3. Should OK, try several times ==");
Console.WriteLine("Restart sql server now, and press any key to continue");
Console.ReadKey();

for (var i = 0; i < 5; i++)
{
    Console.WriteLine($"-- Step 3 >> Attempt {i}");
    try
    {
        result = await client.GetGrain<ITaskGrain>("key").Execute();
        Console.WriteLine(result.Count);
    }
    catch (Exception ex)
    {
        Console.WriteLine(ex.ToString());
    }
    await Task.Delay(TimeSpan.FromSeconds(5));
}

Could you please help on this? Thank you so much.

item.title: Issue: NuGet package conflict for Microsoft.Orleans.Hosting.Kubernetes 3.4.x - @rbitanga-manticore
item.description:
Microsoft.Orleans.Hosting.Kubernetes 3.4.2 does not work with KubernetesClient packages 3.0 or newer due to mismatched method signatures.

System.MissingMethodException: Method not found: 'System.Threading.Tasks.Task`1<k8s.Models.V1PodList> k8s.KubernetesExtensions.ListNamespacedPodAsync(k8s.IKubernetes, System.String, System.Nullable`1<Boolean>, System.String, System.String, System.String, System.Nullable`1<Int32>, System.String, System.Nullable`1<Int32>, System.Nullable`1<Boolean>, System.String, System.Threading.CancellationToken)'.
   at Orleans.Hosting.Kubernetes.KubernetesClusterAgent.OnRuntimeInitializeStart(CancellationToken cancellation)
   at System.Runtime.CompilerServices.AsyncMethodBuilderCore.Start[TStateMachine](TStateMachine& stateMachine)
   at Orleans.Hosting.Kubernetes.KubernetesClusterAgent.OnRuntimeInitializeStart(CancellationToken cancellation)
   at Orleans.LifecycleExtensions.Observer.OnStart(CancellationToken ct)
   at Orleans.Runtime.SiloLifecycleSubject.MonitoredObserver.OnStart(CancellationToken ct)
   at Orleans.LifecycleSubject.OnStart(CancellationToken ct)
   at Orleans.Runtime.Scheduler.AsyncClosureWorkItem.Execute()
   at Orleans.Runtime.Silo.StartAsync(CancellationToken cancellationToken)
   at Orleans.Hosting.SiloHost.StartAsync(CancellationToken cancellationToken)
   at Orleans.Hosting.SiloHostedService.StartAsync(CancellationToken cancellationToken)
   at Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting.Internal.Host.StartAsync(CancellationToken cancellationToken)
   at Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting.HostingAbstractionsHostExtensions.RunAsync(IHost host, CancellationToken token)

            var pods = await _client.ListNamespacedPodAsync(
                namespaceParameter: _podNamespace,
                labelSelector: _podLabelSelector,
                cancellationToken: cancellation);

should work with newer KubernetesClient packages when compiled locally but importing the NuGet package does not work. It looks like a string resourceVersionMatch argument was introduced in between string resourceVersion and int? timeoutSeconds in https://github.com/kubernetes-client/csharp/pull/505.

This shouldn't be an issue with 3.5.0 since that pulls in KubernetesClient 4.0.5 which is based off of Kubernetes 1.19. k8s 1.19 itself is supposed to reach the end of its support window after Aug. 2021 so there's potential for future package conflicts if there are incompatible changes again.

Is there any guidance for how to deal with future package conflicts? Will there be package updates for older Orleans releases as previous Kubernetes versions are no longer supported?

item.title: Issue: Re-building Cluster Clients upon disconnect using the same instance of ClientBuilder - @mihalios
item.description:
I have followed the example given here https://dotnet.github.io/orleans/docs/host/client.html of creating a Cluster Client using the concrete ClientBuilder:

public ClusterClientHostedService(ILoggerProvider loggerProvider)
    {
        Client = new ClientBuilder()
            // Appropriate client configuration here, e.g.:
            .UseLocalhostClustering()
            .ConfigureLogging(builder => builder.AddProvider(loggerProvider))
            .Build();
    }

This works fine, but does make testing a bit cumbersome. I tried taking a different approach:

  1. Register a singleton for IClientBuilder: services.AddSingleton<IClientBuilder, ClientBuilder>();
  2. Inject it using DI: public ClusterClientHostedService(ILoggerProvider loggerProvider, IClientBuilder clientBuilder)
  3. In my hosted service:
public async Task StartAsync(CancellationToken cancellationToken)
{
    while (true)
    {
        try
        {
            Client = _clientBuilder
                .UseLocalhostClustering()
                .Build();
            await Client.Connect();
            break;
        }
        catch (Exception)
        {
            Client.Dispose();
            await Task.Delay(TimeSpan.FromSeconds(10));
        }
    }
}

The problem with this is that .Build() cannot be called more than once on a single instance of ClientBuilder. Clearly this isn't the right approach - can anyone point me in the direction of how this can be done?

item.title: Issue: Queue Mapper only works for static queues? - @wangjia184
item.description:
I am trying to implement a custom stream provider. I guess IConsistentRingStreamQueueMapper.GetQueuesForRange() is called by each silo to determine the queues to be pulled by SystemTargets of current silo. In other words, all queues are distributed on all silos by consistent ring. Is that right?

As I see from the source code, https://github.com/dotnet/orleans/blob/3.5.0/src/Azure/Orleans.Streaming.EventHubs/Providers/Streams/EventHub/EventHubQueueMapper.cs, the queues are static. Does that mean I cannot add more queues when system is running?

In my use case, I want to have 1-to-1 mapping between stream and queue. The queue supports priority. So when producing a message via OnNextAsync, I suppose I can supply the priority within StreamSequenceToken

item.title: Issue: Question: Is IAsyncStream<T> thread safe on OnNextAsync method? - @zeinali0
item.description: All examples in docs used separate instance IAsyncStream<T> per grains which they are single thread themselves. The question is Can we use single instance of IAsyncStream<T> (as producer) out of grains in a multi-thread situation?

item.title: Issue: Architecture Question: Should Orleans Be Used As An Enterprise Bus? - @nkosi23
item.description:
Say an enterprise has 5 independent products written in .NET and needing to pass messages to each other. This is generally done by connecting them to a central enterprise bus. My question is: if all the applications are using Orleans, is it an anti-pattern to use Orleans like an enterprise bus by having the project referencing Orleans.Server be a giant project containing a definition of all the grains of all the applications of the company? Are we supposed to have one Orleans cluster per product, or one Orleans cluster for the whole enterprise? What is the standard practice?

Orleans seems to allow for both scenarios, and Streams allow to pass messages without needing to couple separate applications with each others. All of this while allowing us to have strongly-typed interfaces where needed and appropriate. However I feel this may be too good to be true, and may actually turn out to be an anti-pattern that will bite us down the road. One challenge may be availability during the deployment of application updates to the Orleans server, however clustering may actually have been designed precisely to deal with this kind of scenario. Is it standard/recommended practice?

item.title: Issue: Support Data Contract attributes for serialization - @ReubenBond
item.description: In this comment, @zahirtezcan mentioned a desire for Orleans' serialization to leverage the Data Contract attributes ([DataContract] and [DataMember(Order = x)]) in lieu of the in-built attributes ([GenerateSerializer] and [Id(x)]).

item.title: Issue: Catalog.GCTimer timer should have fired at - @DiAifU
item.description:
Hi,

I have an issue since a few days that I didn't have before. Orleans starts to complain the Catalog.GCTimer has not fired in time after a few minutes/hours after the process start. I haven't had any of these problem before, and I don't know exactly what is causing them, but due to that, the grain starts to respond really slowly. It is working perfectly fine until this message appears for the first time, and then the grain responds slowly. The rest of the process is running fine, because I have some timers that continue to work perfectly fine in the process.

The messages are the following :

Self-monitoring determined that local health is degraded. Degradation score is 1/8 (lower is better). Complaints: "Health check participant Orleans.Runtime.Catalog is reporting that it is unhealthy with complaint: Catalog.GCTimer timer should have fired at 16/06/2021 18:43:19, which is 14:02:52.2339738 ago"

Health check participant "Orleans.Runtime.Catalog" is reporting that it is unhealthy with complaint: "Catalog.GCTimer timer should have fired at 16/06/2021 18:43:19, which is 14:02:52.2339738 ago"

I have both of them at the same time every 10 secs exactly.

I also did a trace in dotTrace but I just see that the process seems to be waiting for something :

Let me know if I can give you more information.

Nicolas

item.title: Issue: Question: What is the execution model of IGrainObserver methods? - @zeinali0
item.description:
According to docs :

Grain activations are single-threaded and, by default, process each request from beginning to completion before the next request can begin processing.

The question is: Does same behavior true for IGrainObserver ? Or IGrainObserver execution is not limited to a single thread. If it limited to single thread is there any way to change it?

item.title: Issue: https://aka.ms/orleans-troubleshooting points to non-existent page - @pechkarus
item.description: While looking at error messages, some of them contained this link https://aka.ms/orleans-troubleshooting. It appears to point to non-existent page (HTTP 404):

item.title: Issue: Can we configure orleans so that SiloHostedService runs after other IHostedServices? - @SebastianStehle
item.description:
Hi,

when you use a simple setup like this

 public static IHostBuilder CreateHostBuilder(string[] args) =>
            Host.CreateDefaultBuilder(args)
                .UseOrleans(...)
                .ConfigureWebHostDefaults(builder =>
                {
                    builder.UseStartup<Startup>();
                });

The SiloHostedService always runs first. Before other services. It is a little bit strange, because when you have a grain with a timer it might start before other services implementing IHostedService are configured.

How can I configure Orleans to run last?

item.description:

After the reboot of a single silo (with a problematic actor) - all problems are gone. Nothing special for these requests wasn't found. Logs:

Current request has been active for 00:23:17.1282598 for activation [Activation: S10.47.128.135:11200:361300094*grn/Wallet.Grains.PlayerGrain/0+905497364-0x9828C6B2@7b6b0ba8 #GrainType=Wallet.Grains.PlayerGrain Placement=RandomPlacement State=Valid NonReentrancyQueueSize=78 EnqueuedOnDispatcher=1 InFlightCount=1 NumRunning=1 IdlenessTimeSpan=00:23:21.5362341 CollectionAgeLimit=00:30:00]. item.title: Issue: Consul Clustering + Kubernetes StatefulSet = bloating membership table? - @wangjia184
item.description:
<PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Orleans.Hosting.Kubernetes" Version="3.4.3" />
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Orleans.OrleansConsulUtils" Version="3.4.3" />
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Orleans.Server" Version="3.4.3" />

siloBuilder
     .UseConsulClustering(opt =>
     {
         opt.Address = new Uri(AppConfig.Orleans.ConsulUrl);
         opt.AclClientToken = AppConfig.Orleans.AclClientToken;
     })
     .UseKubernetesHosting();

          - name: ORLEANS_SERVICE_ID #Required by Orleans 
            valueFrom:
              fieldRef:
                fieldPath: metadata.labels['orleans/serviceId']
          - name: ORLEANS_CLUSTER_ID #Required by Orleans 
            valueFrom:
              fieldRef:
                fieldPath: metadata.labels['orleans/clusterId']
          - name: POD_NAME
            valueFrom:
              fieldRef:
                fieldPath: metadata.labels['statefulset.kubernetes.io/pod-name']
          - name: POD_NAMESPACE
            valueFrom:
              fieldRef:
                fieldPath: metadata.namespace
          - name: POD_IP
            valueFrom:
              fieldRef:
                fieldPath: status.podIP

A StatefulSet with 3 replicas, empty consul. After create the StatefulSet, PODs crashes with following exception.

System.AggregateException: One or more errors occurred. (Failed to get ping responses from 1 of 1 active silos. Newly joining silos validate connectivity with all active silos that have recently updated their 'I Am Alive' value before joining the cluster. Successfully contacted: []. Failed to get response from: [S10.18.123.218:11111:361110184])
 ---> Orleans.Runtime.MembershipService.OrleansClusterConnectivityCheckFailedException: Failed to get ping responses from 1 of 1 active silos. Newly joining silos validate connectivity with all active silos that have recently updated their 'I Am Alive' value before joining the cluster. Successfully contacted: []. Failed to get response from: [S10.18.123.218:11111:361110184]
   at Orleans.Runtime.MembershipService.MembershipAgent.ValidateInitialConnectivity()
   at Orleans.Runtime.MembershipService.MembershipAgent.BecomeActive()
   at Orleans.Runtime.MembershipService.MembershipAgent.<>c__DisplayClass26_0.<<Orleans-ILifecycleParticipant<Orleans-Runtime-ISiloLifecycle>-Participate>g__OnBecomeActiveStart|6>d.MoveNext()
--- End of stack trace from previous location where exception was thrown ---
   at Orleans.Runtime.SiloLifecycleSubject.MonitoredObserver.OnStart(CancellationToken ct)
   at Orleans.LifecycleSubject.OnStart(CancellationToken ct)
   at Orleans.Runtime.Scheduler.AsyncClosureWorkItem.Execute()
   at Orleans.Runtime.Silo.StartAsync(CancellationToken cancellationToken)
   at Orleans.Hosting.SiloHost.StartAsync(CancellationToken cancellationToken)
   at Orleans.Hosting.SiloHostedService.StartAsync(CancellationToken cancellationToken)
   at Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting.Internal.Host.StartAsync(CancellationToken cancellationToken)
   at Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting.HostingAbstractionsHostExtensions.RunAsync(IHost host, CancellationToken token)
   at Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting.HostingAbstractionsHostExtensions.RunAsync(IHost host, CancellationToken token)
   at UBS.OrleansServer.EntryPoint.Start() in /app/UBS/OrleansServer/EntryPoint.cs:line 102
   --- End of inner exception stack trace ---

When Pods crash, StatefulSet restarts them. After some time, they stablize down. Note the RESTARTS column

NAME                                 READY   STATUS             RESTARTS   AGE
ubs-job-dev-0                        1/1     Running            4          17m
ubs-job-dev-1                        1/1     Running            4          16m
ubs-job-dev-2                        1/1     Running            3          16m

Logs says there are 7 silos

ProcessTableUpdate (called from TryUpdateMyStatusGlobalOnce) membership table: 7 silos, 3 are Active, 4 are Dead, Version=<33, 31015>. All silos: [SiloAddress=S10.18.123.246:11111:361178481 SiloName=ubs-job-dev-0 Status=Active, SiloAddress=S10.18.123.199:11111:361178519 SiloName=ubs-job-dev-1 Status=Active, SiloAddress=S10.18.117.114:11111:361178416 SiloName=ubs-job-dev-2 Status=Active, SiloAddress=S10.18.117.114:11111:361178292 SiloName=ubs-job-dev-2 Status=Dead, SiloAddress=S10.18.123.199:11111:361178366 SiloName=ubs-job-dev-1 Status=Dead, SiloAddress=S10.18.123.235:11111:361177868 SiloName=ubs-job-dev-0 Status=Dead, SiloAddress=S10.18.123.246:11111:361178329 SiloName=ubs-job-dev-0 Status=Dead]

Unlike ReplicaSet, the pod name in StatefulSet does not change after restart. But it seems Orleans see each newly-started pod as a new silo, even its pod name does not change after restart.

And Consul gets more and more keys when PODs restart.

Although the POD will eventually stablize down and works well. But every time when our CI tool performs RollUpgrade on StatefulSet, the PODs keep crashing and need restart over and over again to startup.

With only 3 replicas, it bloats to more than 20 silos in membership table. Will the Dead record removed from Consul sometime later? or it will bloat forever?

Received cluster membership snapshot via gossip: [Version: 56, 21 silos, SiloAddress=S10.18.123.199:11111:361178366 SiloName=ubs-job-dev-1 Status=Dead, SiloAddress=S10.18.123.199:11111:361178235 SiloName=ubs-job-dev-1 Status=Dead, SiloAddress=S10.18.123.199:11111:361178120 SiloName=ubs-job-dev-1 Status=Dead, SiloAddress=S10.18.123.199:11111:361178519 SiloName=ubs-job-dev-1 Status=Dead, SiloAddress=S10.18.123.199:11111:361178018 SiloName=ubs-job-dev-1 Status=Dead, SiloAddress=S10.18.123.227:11111:361186453 SiloName=ubs-job-dev-1 Status=Active, SiloAddress=S10.18.123.246:11111:361178201 SiloName=ubs-job-dev-0 Status=Dead, SiloAddress=S10.18.123.246:11111:361178329 SiloName=ubs-job-dev-0 Status=Dead, SiloAddress=S10.18.123.246:11111:361178481 SiloName=ubs-job-dev-0 Status=Dead, SiloAddress=S10.18.123.246:11111:361177984 SiloName=ubs-job-dev-0 Status=Dead, SiloAddress=S10.18.123.246:11111:361178087 SiloName=ubs-job-dev-0 Status=Dead, SiloAddress=S10.18.117.114:11111:361178075 SiloName=ubs-job-dev-2 Status=Dead, SiloAddress=S10.18.117.114:11111:361178416 SiloName=ubs-job-dev-2 Status=Dead, SiloAddress=S10.18.117.114:11111:361178174 SiloName=ubs-job-dev-2 Status=Dead, SiloAddress=S10.18.117.114:11111:361178292 SiloName=ubs-job-dev-2 Status=Dead, SiloAddress=S10.18.123.197:11111:361186514 SiloName=ubs-job-dev-0 Status=Active, SiloAddress=S10.18.117.116:11111:361182925 SiloName=ubs-job-dev-2 Status=Dead, SiloAddress=S10.18.123.235:11111:361177868 SiloName=ubs-job-dev-0 Status=Dead, SiloAddress=S10.18.123.218:11111:361182980 SiloName=ubs-job-dev-1 Status=Dead, SiloAddress=S10.18.123.255:11111:361183031 SiloName=ubs-job-dev-0 Status=Dead, SiloAddress=S10.18.117.117:11111:361186385 SiloName=ubs-job-dev-2 Status=Active] info: Orleans.Runtime.MembershipService.ClusterHealthMonitor[100612]

item.title: Issue: Remove GrainService and provide a better alternative - @ReubenBond
item.description:
GrainServices are cumbersome and undocumented. They derive from SystemTarget, which is meant only for internal services and was not meant to be publicly exposed. Hence, I think we should remove them.

A preferable alternative would be to have regular grains which have fixed, per-silo placement. Such grains can still include consistent hash rings and a similar kind of "grain service client" which maps the caller's grain id to that hash ring to find the correct grain service can also be implemented.

item.title: Issue: Documentation: error in code example - @cjenp
item.description:
Hi,

there is a small bug in the Clients documentation: https://dotnet.github.io/orleans/docs/host/client.html

If you look at the code example for changing GatewayListRefreshPeriod, currently it is .Configure<GatewayOptions>(opts => GatewayListRefreshPeriod = TimeSpan.FromMinutes(10)) should be .Configure<GatewayOptions>(opts => opts.GatewayListRefreshPeriod = TimeSpan.FromMinutes(10))

item.title: Issue: How Orleans can be used in an IoT project? - @staviloglu
item.description:
Hi.

First of all thanks for the Orleans framework. I appreciate your efforts to make distributed application development a lot easier.

Orleans seems to be an exact match for IoT business where we need device twins on backend.

But unfortunately I can not seem to find a suitable example rather than the old GPS Tracker example which is working with an HTTP Frontend.

I was wondering if you could point me in the right direction and your thoughts on the following architectures that come to my mind.

I will use RabbitMQ and its MQTT Plugin to support MQTT connections from the devices on the field. They will periodically publish sensor data to given topics.

1- Each grain can create its own connection + channel and subscribe/publish to related queue on RabbitMQ. This does not seem to be a reasonable solution to me, because each device already makes connections if the device twins do that too we double the connection count which will lead to performance loss on RabbitMQ.

2- Each silo will have 2 connections (one for publish and one for subscribe) but each grain will create their own channels using the shared connections. This solution is ok for connection count but increase in channel count creates a lot less but almost the same effect on RabbitMQ. And how should I share the connections (1 stateless worker, IHostedService, GrainService?) Pooling connections come to my mind but first need to be sure.

3- I may create different processes for subscribing and publishing, each create their own connections and subscribe/publish to RabbitMQ. When a message arrived I can use GrainClient to send data to Orleans cluster. That seems totally fine to me, this way I can scale subscription process seperate from the silos. But I'm not sure how can I publish from grains to RabbitMQ which requires orleans to communicate with the publisher process. (gRPC etc? Orleans streams?)

4- I may have the subscriber process mentioned above but rather than a seperate publisher process, each grain can create their connection + channel and publish the data when required. And close the connection + cannel. Or share one connection per silo but seperate channel opened/closed in each grain.

5- Stream Providers? I have seen some documentation about pulling agents but I want to use RabbitMQ push based. I have seen Stream provider for RabbitMQ bu I think that does not solve the problem here. Am I wrong?

Tihs is might be too much to ask and time consuming to read. :(

Hope to see an answer from your side.

Thanks for your time.

item.title: Issue: [EPIC] Papercuts to fix for 4.0.0 - @ReubenBond
item.description:
This is an issue to log and track papercuts: small annoyances/inconveniences which developers often run into. Please feel free to add to the list and to pick off items on the list to fix.

  • [ ] Silo shutdown takes too long, even in dev/test scenarios with a single silo and no persistence
  • [ ] Logging noise: MemoryStorageGrain grain logs OnActivateAsync message
  • [ ] Logging noise: On shutdown, silo spews a lot of information which is not meaningful to developers
  • [ ] Logging noise: Gateway refresh notifications on client are unnecessary
  • [ ] Logging noise: Periodic statistics dumps are unnecessary / unexpected
  • [ ] Logging noise: deployment load publisher
  • [ ] Logging noise: Logging that CleanupDefunctSiloEntries is not supported is unnecessary, especially as a warning. We should also not throw an exception and should implement it on most/all providers
  • [ ] Logging noise: "No implementation of IHostEnvironmentStatistics was found. Load shedding will not work yet"
    • Add config validator to only warn/error if LoadSheddingOptions.LoadSheddingEnabled is true
  • [ ] Logging noise: VirtualBucketsRingProvider
  • [ ] ClusterId & ServiceId should have a configured default, eg "default"
  • [ ] ISiloBuilder vs ISiloHostBuilder is confusing - delete SiloHostBuilder entirely and steer everyone towards generic host
  • [ ] The distinction between Grain<T> and Grain with IPersistentState<T> injected could be confusing - we should consider removing Grain<T>
  • [x] Application Parts should never need configuring (Fixed in #7070 by removing app parts entirely)

item.title: Issue: Question: Any news about running Orleans on Azure App Service - @christiansparre
item.description:
Hi, in the gitter chat and in the On .NET Deep dive video (timestamped to the mention) back in march @ReubenBond mentioned that it should now be possible to run Orleans on Azure App Service (something that was not possible before) and that it was being used by teams internal at Microsoft.

I have not been able to find more information about this (I might have missed more gitter conversations about it), but it is a very interesting notion to our team because jumping into the whole Kubernetes/Service fabric world might be too big of a shift in technology etc.

We have use cases where Orleans might be an absolutely fantastic fit, but putting Kubernetes or Service Fabric on top might not make it feasible currently.

I'm wondering if I might have misunderstood or that information about it is just not ready to be shared yet?

item.title: Issue: Orleans CodeGenerator (MSBuilld) fails when previous code has a static local function - @icanhasjonas
item.description:
Given this code


namespace SomeNamespace.Grains {
	public class A {
		private IEventFactory _eventFactory;

		public Task Hello() => _eventFactory
			.BeginUpdate("hello")
			.Scope(() => {
				ValidateReferenceIntegrity(null, Guid.Empty, null);

				static void ValidateReferenceIntegrity(string existingGrain, Guid givenValue, string parameterName)
				{
					if (existingGrain is not null && existingGrain == null) {
						throw new Exception(parameterName);
					}
				}

				return Task.CompletedTask;
			})
			.ProcessAsync();
	}

	public class AState { }
}

Expected: The generated code should register the AState object as a known type on the namespace SomeNamespace.Grains but it registers it as <global namespace>.AState

If you remove the static void ValidateReferenceIntegrity (just comment it out) it will generate the correct code again. It is almost like the code syntax parser (Roslyn?) is using an older syntax version and failing to parse AState as part of SomeNamespace.Grains namespace.

This obviously breaks code that tries to deserialize AState from persisted storage etc.

PS. Moving AState to the top of the file also fixes the issue, again reinforcing the idea that this is a roslyn syntax issue (possibly an older roslyn reference)

item.title: Issue: How to use postgres with SSL enabled to become AdoNetClustering - @coocms
item.description:
Hi guys, Our company recently required all interactions with the database to use two-way authentication. Does Orleans provide relevant interfaces to configure database certificates? Or is there any way to get the DBConnection inside Orleans? I have cleared how to implement mutual authentication after obtaining the DBconnection object.

This is the error message：Exception while performing SSL handshake

item.title: Issue: SimpleMemoryStream OnNextAsync cancellation - @highlyunavailable
item.description: I'm trying to use SimpleMemoryStream to send data to an Orleans Client that is on a frontend server. I've found that in testing, if I kill off the frontend server without cleanly unsubscribing, calls to OnNextAsync hang infinitely (at least more than 5 minutes! I didn't test past that) and as such it blocks the grain that called OnNextAsync to deliver an event. I can use WithTimeout to bypass that but I'm afraid the task is leaking, since there's no way to actually cancel the call, and it never appears to fault on its own. Any idea what I can do to make sure that a "failed" client doesn't block streams or leak tasks? Alternately, is this a side effect of local testing vs. having an actual cluster directory?

item.title: Issue: OrleansBrokenTransactionLockException when read and write in the same grain method - @luyangdev
item.description:
Hello, I got OrleansBrokenTransactionLockException when:

  • Silo: PerformRead and PerformUpdate in the same grain method.
  • Client: make multiple calls of the grain method above at the same time

I've marked the grain class [Reentrant] as discussed in #5644

Reproduce the issue: github repo

Exception:

Unhandled exception. Orleans.Transactions.OrleansBrokenTransactionLockException: 
Transaction 238e27ee-1cf1-4e3f-9a12-c7da6045541f aborted because a broken lock was detected, when trying to re-enter lock
at Orleans.Internal.OrleansTaskExtentions.<ToTypedTask>g__ConvertAsync|4_0[T](Task`1 asyncTask)

Silo grain method:

async Task<GrainState> ITaskGrain.Execute()
{
           var state = await _state.PerformRead(_ => _);
           state.Count++;
           await _state.PerformUpdate(e => e.Count = state.Count);
           return state;
}

Client:

var tasks = Enumerable.Range(1, 100).Select(async _ => await _client.GetGrain<ITaskGrain>("key").Execute()).ToList();
var results = await Task.WhenAll(tasks)

item.title: Issue: Is it possible for multilpe activations of the same grain to be called for a single request - @bruno-goncalves-cko
item.description:
Hi,

If a cluster happens to have two activations of the same grain on two different silos, could a single request end up being routed to both activations or would that be impossible? I.e., in a multi activation scenario is it guaranteed that only one of the activations will handle a request?

Thanks in advance

item.title: Issue: Multiple activations of same grain - @mradovcic
item.description:
Hi all,

I have an issue where multiple activations of same grain are activated even when there is only one silo in the cluster Grain code is here

    public interface IActivityLookupGrain : IGrainWithStringKey
    {
        [AlwaysInterleave]
        Task<Guid?> GetSubscribedUser();

        Task SubscribeUser(Guid userId);
        Task UnsubscribeUser(Guid userId);
        Task Deactivate();
    }

    [PreferLocalPlacement]
    public class ActivityLookupGrain : Grain, IActivityLookupGrain
    {
        private Guid? State;

        public ActivityLookupGrain()
        {
            State = null;
        }

        public override async Task OnActivateAsync()
        {
            Console.WriteLine($"Activating ActivityLookupGrain {this.GetPrimaryKeyString()} {DateTime.UtcNow}");
            this.DelayDeactivation(TimeSpan.MaxValue);
            await base.OnActivateAsync();
        }

        public override Task OnDeactivateAsync()
        {
           Console.WriteLine($"Dectivating ActivityLookupGrain {this.GetPrimaryKeyString()} {DateTime.UtcNow}");
            return base.OnDeactivateAsync();
        }

        public Task<Guid?> GetSubscribedUser()
        {
            Console.WriteLine($"GetSubscribedUser ActivityLookupGrain {this.GetPrimaryKeyString()} {DateTime.UtcNow}");
            return Task.FromResult(State);
        }

        public Task SubscribeUser(Guid userId)
        {
            Console.WriteLine($"SubscribeUser ActivityLookupGrain {this.GetPrimaryKeyString()} {DateTime.UtcNow}");
            State = userId;
            return Task.CompletedTask;
        }

        public Task UnsubscribeUser(Guid userId)
        {
            if (State.GetValueOrDefault() == userId)
                State = null;
            return Task.CompletedTask;
        }

        public Task Deactivate()
        {
            Console.WriteLine($"Deactivate ActivityLookupGrain {this.GetPrimaryKeyString()} {DateTime.UtcNow}");
            this.DeactivateOnIdle();
            return Task.CompletedTask;
        }
    }

it is a very simple grain that is used only to follow which user (if any) is subscribed to follow changes that are sent as notifications from other grain.

// UserGrain    

public async Task NotificationSubscribe(Guid id1, Guid id2, Guid id3)
{
	var key = KeyHelper.ConstructKey(id1, id2, id3); // $"{id1}_{id2}_{id3}
	await GrainFactory.GetGrain<IActivityLookupGrain>(key).SubscribeUser(this.GetPrimaryKey());
	await GrainFactory.GetGrain<IWorkerGrain>(key).Initialize();
}

//WorkerGrain
public async Task Initialize()
{
	//...some logic...
	var key = this.GetPrimaryKeyString();
	string notificationUserId = (await GrainFactory.GetGrain<IActivityLookupGrain>(key).GetSubscribedUser()).ToString();
	if (String.IsNullOrEmpty(notificationUserId))
	{
		_logger.WriteWarning("Activity lookup grain - Empty userId.");
		return;
	}
	//...send init success notification
}

And this is what i get in a console

Activating ActivityLookupGrain 679a5ac9-d5da-4c9e-9dd2-dcf2eb07cdbc_bd4439af-29c3-2fdb-ed40-78fe07f61215_f73fd581-a23c-40b5-b9b9-ba43ddfa1818 5/17/2021 4:35:07 PM
SubscribeUser ActivityLookupGrain 679a5ac9-d5da-4c9e-9dd2-dcf2eb07cdbc_bd4439af-29c3-2fdb-ed40-78fe07f61215_f73fd581-a23c-40b5-b9b9-ba43ddfa1818 5/17/2021 4:35:07 PM
Activating ActivityLookupGrain 679a5ac9-d5da-4c9e-9dd2-dcf2eb07cdbc_bd4439af-29c3-2fdb-ed40-78fe07f61215_f73fd581-a23c-40b5-b9b9-ba43ddfa1818 5/17/2021 4:35:08 PM

item.title: Issue: Error CS8669 in generated code for grain interface with method retuning a nullable C# record - @Arshia001
item.description:
I'm using Orleans version 3.4.2 with the CodeGenerator.MSBuild package. This is my interface:

    public interface IPlayer : IGrainWithGuidKey
    {
        Task<PlayerInfo?> GetInfo();
    }

In which PlayerInfo is a simple DTO record with a parameterless constructor. The code generator generates this code:

global::System.Threading.Tasks.Task<PlayerInfo?> global::XXXXXX.Player.IPlayer.GetInfo()
{
    return base.InvokeMethodAsync<PlayerInfo?>(unchecked((int)0xBA53CC69), null);
}

Which fails to compile due to:

CS8669	The annotation for nullable reference types should only be used in code within a '#nullable' annotations context. Auto-generated code requires an explicit '#nullable' directive in source.

I also very highly suspect that System.Threading.Tasks.Task<PlayerInfo?> is wrong and should use a fully qualified name like System.Threading.Tasks.Task<global::XXXXXX.Player.PlayerInfo?>.

Edit: Changing the record into a class fixes the fully qualified name problem. It also fixes the nullable problem:

global::System.Threading.Tasks.Task<global::XXXXXX.Player.PlayerInfo> global::XXXXXX.Player.IPlayer.GetInfo()
        {
            return base.InvokeMethodAsync<global::XXXXXX.Player.PlayerInfo>(unchecked((int)0xBA53CC69), null);
        }

so I'm guessing the actual problem is with the code generator not recognizing records.

If I had to guess, I'd say it's because the C# compiler version used by the code generator is old and doesn't support records.

item.title: Issue: AddDynamoDBGrainStorage - not working as expected - @PiotrJustyna
item.description:
I'm having a difficult time getting AddDynamoDBGrainStorage working the way it is documented:

https://dotnet.github.io/orleans/docs/grains/grain_persistence/dynamodb_storage.html

Here is the code which specifically does not work for me in some scenarios:

siloBuilder.AddDynamoDBGrainStorage(
    name: "helloWorldStore",
    configureOptions: options =>
    {
        options.UseJson = true;
        options.AccessKey = accessKey;
        options.SecretKey = secretKey;
        options.Service = "us-west-2";
    });

What is not working? There are two scenarios which result in exceptions:

| - | AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY and AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID environment variables present | AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY and AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID environment variables missing | | --- | --- | ---| | correct values of options.AccessKey and options.SecretKey provided | This scenario is not relevant to me and I didn't check what's happening in this case. | The security token included in the request is invalid. More on this error below. | | options.AccessKey and options.SecretKey not provided | dynamodb connection works | Unable to get IAM security credentials from EC2 Instance Metadata Service. I would expect DynamoDBStorage to refer to my ~/.aws/credentials for the expected values (which are present there by the way). |

Maybe I'm missing something, but I'm not sure this is the way the DynamoDBStorage should work. For reference, at the time of writing this, the DynamoDBStorage looks as follows:

private void CreateClient()
{
    if (this.service.StartsWith("http://", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase) ||
        this.service.StartsWith("https://", StringComparison.OrdinalIgnoreCase))
    {
        // Local DynamoDB instance (for testing)
        var credentials = new BasicAWSCredentials("dummy", "dummyKey");
        this.ddbClient = new AmazonDynamoDBClient(credentials, new AmazonDynamoDBConfig { ServiceURL = this.service });
    }
    else if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(this.accessKey) && !string.IsNullOrEmpty(this.secretKey))
    {
        // AWS DynamoDB instance (auth via explicit credentials)
        var credentials = new BasicAWSCredentials(this.accessKey, this.secretKey);
        this.ddbClient = new AmazonDynamoDBClient(credentials, new AmazonDynamoDBConfig {RegionEndpoint = AWSUtils.GetRegionEndpoint(this.service)});
    }
    else
    {
        // AWS DynamoDB instance (implicit auth - EC2 IAM Roles etc)
        this.ddbClient = new AmazonDynamoDBClient(new AmazonDynamoDBConfig {RegionEndpoint = AWSUtils.GetRegionEndpoint(this.service)});
    }
}

  1. Condition 1 is fine for local use.

  2. Condition 2 (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(this.accessKey) && !string.IsNullOrEmpty(this.secretKey)) results in the code creating an instance of BasicAWSCredentials which behind the scenes results in:

public BasicAWSCredentials(string accessKey, string secretKey)
{
  if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(accessKey))
    return;
  this._credentials = new ImmutableCredentials(accessKey, secretKey, (string) null);
}

Where (string) null is the token, which to me explains why we're getting the The security token included in the request is invalid. exception (correct me if I'm wrong!). Shouldn't we be passing in the token as well?

  1. Condition 3 is fine for production use.

  2. What seems to be missing is ~/.aws/credentials file fallback option. Once AWS-authenticated, user credentials are stored there by default.

Are these conditions there by design? Do we want to ignore the token and the default credentials file?

item.title: Issue: Support C# records in grain method parameters and return value - @VincentH-Net
item.description:
As discussed with @ReubenBond here Adding support for C# records in Orleans method parameters would allow this code

[Immutable]
public class CallParty
{
    public string Name { get; init; }
    public string Address { get; init; }
    public string UserId  { get; init; }
}

To become this

[Immutable]
public record CallParty(string Name, string Address, string UserId);

In addition, records eliminate writing code for value equality, hash code - many loc saved.

The support should enable to use the record CallParty and immutable collections of that, like ImmutableArray<CallParty>, in grain method signatures.

Eliminating the need to add the [Immutable] attribute to records would be a nice to have. Roslyn code generators should allow access to the actual source text, so even if the Roslyn model does not distinguish records, it should be possible to determine that from the source code text.

The combination of [Immutable] and record would be an reasonably safe performance optimization, provided any contained collections are immutable, e.g. ImmutableArray<T>. For calls within the same silo no deep copy will be made, while the state of the calling actor will also be reasonably well protected from unintentional external modification by the (adequate) immutability of record and ImmutableArray<T> (or equivalent).

Possible related issues: #6822, #7033

item.title: Issue: Reminders fireing before reminder service is initialized - @Jubast
item.description:
Setup

I am using Orleans 3.3.0 with Microsoft.Orleans.Reminders.AzureStorage reminders and Orleankka 3.4.0. When the silo is starting I have a IStartupTask that activates a few grains which Register reminders that fire periodically.

public override async Task OnActivate()
{
    if (!await Reminders.IsRegistered(MyReminderName))
        await Reminders.Register(MyReminderName, TimeSpan.FromSeconds(30), TimeSpan.FromDays(1));

    await base.OnActivate();
}

Initial issue

NOTE: The issues bellow started happening after we added AzureServiceBus Subscriber services to the silo. The nuget is https://www.nuget.org/packages/Azure.Messaging.ServiceBus/7.1.2. But I didn't find any conflicts in nuget dependencies, that's why I decided to open a issue here.

The first thing that occurred was a NRE at silo startup

System.NullReferenceException:
   at Orleans.Runtime.ReminderService.AzureBasedReminderTable+<ReadRow>d__14.MoveNext (Orleans.Reminders.AzureStorage, Version=2.0.0.0, Culture=neutral, PublicKeyToken=null)

After debugging a bit more I discovered that adding a delay before reminders start fixed the issue.

public override async Task OnActivate()
{
    await Task.Delay(2000);
    if (!await Reminders.IsRegistered(MyReminderName))
        await Reminders.Register(MyReminderName, TimeSpan.FromSeconds(30), TimeSpan.FromDays(1));

    await base.OnActivate();
}

Second issue

After checking the logs I also found out that reminders started triggered multiple times. (We have a cluster setup of 3 silos if this information helps)

5/6/2021, 10:45:48 AM - CUSTOM EVENT MyReminder started at {now}
5/6/2021, 10:45:48 AM - CUSTOM EVENT MyReminder started at {now}
5/6/2021, 10:46:34 AM - CUSTOM EVENT MyReminder finished at {now}
5/6/2021, 10:46:35 AM - CUSTOM EVENT MyReminder finished at {now}

The code looks like this:

public override async Task OnReminder(string id)
{
    if (id == MyReminderName)
    {
#pragma warning disable 4014
        MyReminderHandler();
#pragma warning restore 4014
    }

    await base.OnReminder(id);
}

Basically the reminder starts a Task that does some async work which normally takes more than 30 seconds (that's why the call is not awaited). So the reminder exists instantly.

After some more debugging I found out that duplicate triggering can be prevented if I add a delay before exiting the reminder.

public override async Task OnReminder(string id)
{
    if (id == MyReminderName)
    {
#pragma warning disable 4014
        MyReminderHandler();
#pragma warning restore 4014

        await Task.Delay(5000);
    }

    await base.OnReminder(id);
}

Null issue (initial issue)

Today I decided to find out what is happening and discovered that there is some kind of race condition in the LocalReminderService. At silo startup the LocalReminderService code is called in the following way:

  • LocalReminderService.Start is called but is somehow not awaited
  • LocalReminderService.GetReminder is called
  • NRE occurs in AzureBasedReminderTable.ReadRow

I saw that the LocalReminderService.Start Task can be canceled with a timeout from ClusterMembershipOptions.MaxJoinAttemptTime . But we already set the timeout to 15min. And the NRE occurs in the first 20sec of the silo start.

Here is the ClusterMembershipOptions configuration:

siloHostBuilder.Configure<ClusterMembershipOptions>(options =>
{
    options.MaxJoinAttemptTime = TimeSpan.FromMinutes(15);
    options.ProbeTimeout = TimeSpan.FromSeconds(5);
    options.DeathVoteExpirationTimeout = TimeSpan.FromMinutes(2);
    options.TableRefreshTimeout = TimeSpan.FromSeconds(5);
    options.NumMissedProbesLimit = 3;
    options.NumVotesForDeathDeclaration = 2;
})

Currently a fix that works for me is adding a 5 second delay before activating Reminder Grains in IStartupTask.

I hope you can help me. I am out of ideas what could be wrong :/

item.title: Issue: ManagementGrain returns cached membership table silo status - @awshrubsall
item.description:
I am looking at the Health Check Sample found here: https://github.com/dotnet/orleans/blob/main/Samples/2.3/HealthCheckSample/src/Silo/ClusterHealthCheck.cs trying to implement a health service on Orleans v3.4.1.

I have noticed that GetHosts on the ManagementGrain never changes result after startup, and I believe it is due to the cache in the SiloStatusOracle. https://github.com/dotnet/orleans/blob/10af0f4af588cd4aa45cb3e250dfbffa389d59c7/src/Orleans.Runtime/MembershipService/SiloStatusOracle.cs#L49-L54 If I create a situation where a silo in the membership table is gracefully terminated, it marks its Status "Dead", and I start a new silo process, the ClusterHealthCheck here reports a degraded service with "1 silo(s) unavailable". I can see a debug log after membershipTableManager.Refresh() is called:

[15:50:08 DBG] ProcessTableUpdate (called from Refresh) membership table 2 silos, 1 are Active, 1 are Dead, Version=<16, W/"datetime'2021-05-07T03%3A49%3A29.2200842Z'">. All silos: [SiloAddress=S10.8.226.129:11111:358055395 SiloName=Silo_7863a Status=Active, SiloAddress=S10.8.226.129:11111:358055107 SiloName=Silo_1882c Status=Dead]

Later when this dead silo is cleared out of the membership table, I can see another debug log from the MembershipTableManager

[15:50:42 DBG] ProcessTableUpdate (called from Refresh) membership table 1 silos, 1 are Active, 0 are Dead, Version=<17, W/"datetime'2021-05-07T03%3A49%3A59.7370000Z'">. All silos: [SiloAddress=S10.8.226.129:11111:358055395 SiloName=Silo_7863a Status=Active]

Yet ManagementGrain.GetHosts() still has the dead silo in its list and so the ClusterHealthCheck never progresses from Degraded to Healthy.

I am using Azure Table Storage for my cluster management, if that makes a difference, I haven't tried this on any other implementation.

item.title: Issue: Save state without using the serialize attribute - @nkosi23
item.description:
Hello,

I am new to Orleans and have been digging into the documentation for quite a few weeks now to use it in a major project.

I have two questions:

  1. Is it possible to use state persistence without marking the state class as serializable? (for example, using a custom storage provider)
  2. Is it acceptable/safe to store state without using orleans' built-in state management infrastructure (for example by manually managing database repositories from inside a grain class)

To provide context, we practice Domain Driven Design, and as such thrive to keep the domain model pure of any infrastructure-related concern (such as attributes and annotation to help the persistence layer). Generally speaking, we cannot modify the domain model.

What we'd like to do is use Orleans in the following manner:

  1. Create rich domain model classes, for example a User class
  2. Create a generic MyApplicationGrain class, having a property public T DomainObject { get; }
  3. In the OnActivate method, load the DomainObject property from the database (or let the built-in state management mechanism do so)
  4. In our application code, get a grain reference and then call directly MyApplicationGrain.DomainObject.SomeMethodOrProperty() to run our business logic (without defining an interface at the Grain level)
  5. Call a generic Save method on our generic Grain class

Our goal is to avoid duplicating the logic of the domain model in the Grain Layer (and similarly to avoid duplicating the data model in the Grain Layer by having to create custom persistence objects). With this approach the Grain layer would be very thin (only containing a single generic grain class).

What we'd still get from Orleans is concurrency management and the ability to assume that only one instance of the grain is loaded across nodes (which is a game changing guarantee), as well the ability to use advance features such streams and reminders in an opt-in manner.

So beyond the two questions above, I am also trying to understand if our intended usage would be an antipattern, and if Orleans was meant to be used in this manner.

Thanks you

item.title: Issue: [Help Need]: Orleans Multi Cluster Deployments - @doctorcode-org

https://github.com/dotnet/orleans/releases/tag/v3.2.0

What is the recommended approach to hosting two clusters and allowing the Orleans clusters to communicate?

My application will need to ensure that a guid Grain on cluster A is not getting acted upon at the same time as a Grain in cluster B with the same guid.

item.title: Issue: Redis Grain Directory does not remove the key in Redis if the silo address was cleaned from the membership table - @VladislavRybnikov
item.description:
Hi,

We are using Orleans 3.3.0 with Redis grain directory and Consul membership table provider and observed some issues with actors trying to activate on the silo that was removed from Consul after default cleanup period (7 days).

Example of exception:

No target activation S10.207.149.18:11200:357113337*cli/4a504b8a@acf866bd for response message: Transient Rejection (info: Exception while sending message: Orleans.Runtime.Messaging.ConnectionFailedException: Unable to connect to endpoint S10.47.130.160:11200:355373853. See InnerException
 ---> System.Threading.Tasks.TaskCanceledException: A task was canceled.
   at Orleans.Internal.OrleansTaskExtentions.MakeCancellable[T](Task`1 task, CancellationToken cancellationToken)
   at Orleans.Runtime.Messaging.ConnectionManager.ConnectAsync(SiloAddress address)
   --- End of inner exception stack trace ---
   at Orleans.Runtime.Messaging.ConnectionManager.ConnectAsync(SiloAddress address)
   at Orleans.Runtime.Messaging.ConnectionManager.GetConnectionAsync(SiloAddress endpoint)
   at Orleans.Runtime.Messaging.OutboundMessageQueue.<SendMessage>g__SendAsync|10_0(ValueTask`1 c, Message m)) Response S10.47.130.160:11200:355373853*grn/16567B52/00000000+609334678@86b569bb->S10.207.149.18:11200:357113337*cli/4a504b8a@acf866bd #66830793

In our application, the case when the actor will be reactivated next time after a long period of time (several months) are very often and non-deterministic, also we have frequent deploys so this problem is very relevant for us.

I saw the discussion inside issue 7054 but didn't find any solution.

  1. Do we really need to extend DefunctSiloCleanupPeriod to some long period (the year for example) to avoid such situations?
  2. Maybe it is possible to handle such exceptions from the client code and clean up keys manually?
  3. Also, maybe there is a possibility to fix this from the Orleans side? For example, change an algorithm of determining that the silo is known to be dead or handle connection errors by removing the key from Redis after multiple transient exceptions.

Thank you very much, and looking for your reply.

item.title: Issue: Adding new properties in already persisted grain model gives error - @saurabhtyagi00
item.description:
I am persisting a grain with the help of ADO persistent. It is working fine. Now i add some new properties in the Grain Model and try to run it again, but it is giving me some casting error -

Error reading grain state: ServiceId=serviceid ProviderName=modelStore GrainType=Flow.Implementations.Trigger.ScheduledTriggerGrain,Flow.Implementations.scheduleState GrainId=0#a70bd631-b101-4413-907f-3fae19a47e3d ETag= Exception=Unable to cast object of type 'System.Boolean' to type 'System.Int32'.. System.InvalidCastException: Unable to cast object of type 'System.Boolean' to type 'System.Int32'.

I debug it further and found Flow.Abstraction.orleans.g.cs file with method public global::System.Object Deserializer(global::System.Type expected, global::Orleans.Serialization.IDeserializationContext context) causing this error.

Actually all the properties are stored in ascending order in persistence db and they retrieved in the same order. if we add new property it will be get/set according to the order. it is causing me error in previously persisted data.

How can i check and clear this type of previous persisted data or any other way

item.description:

item.title: Issue: Method to cleanly cordon/drain/evict grains from a silo? - @highlyunavailable
item.description: Is there a way to cleanly migrate off of a silo? The reason I ask is because I am doing deployments with Helm to a set of Orleans silos on Kubernetes, but simply asking the silos to shutdown normally causes all sorts of havoc like clients asking for Non-Existent Activations. I'm waiting for a clean shutdown and all that, but is there a way to tell Orelans explicitly "Get all running grains off of this silo and make sure no grains can activate there in the future, because I'm going to be stopping it soon"? Especially reminders and the like - it seems like there's no good way to drain a silo before stopping it.

item.title: Issue: Orleans Streams throws: Operation is not valid due to the current state of the object. in .NET5 host - @rjygraham
item.description:
The current Microsoft.Orleans.OrleansServiceBus package has a dependency on an older version of Microsoft.Azure.EventHubs which is incompatible with .NET5.

When running the SiloHost, you will encounter an exception similar to:

System.InvalidOperationException
  HResult=0x80131509
  Message=Operation is not valid due to the current state of the object.
  Source=Microsoft.Azure.Amqp
  StackTrace:
   at Microsoft.Azure.Amqp.Transport.TransportStream.Flush()

This has been detailed in another GH issue: https://github.com/Azure/azure-amqp/issues/165

Explicitly adding the latest version (currently 4.3.1) of Microsoft.Azure.EventHubs package resolves this exception.

item.title: Issue: Transactions and multiple call to the same grains - @StephaneMartignier
item.description:
Hi everyone,

First of all congrats for the work Orleans, it's really nice to use it.

So I try to use the transactions to achieve the following: I built a system that allow to migrate some database based on xml files. To achieve this I have three grains : IMigrateDataBaseGrain: Open the transaction (method marked as [Transaction(TransactionOption.Create)]), build the sql and while building call ISqlDataSourcesGrain to apply changes. When finishing building the SQL, execute it inside a (sql) transaction. ISqlDataSourcesGrain Contains the list of existing tables (in the state which is a custom object). Methods are marked as [Transaction(TransactionOption.CreateOrJoin)]. Call ISqlDataSourceGrain ISqlDataSourceGrain Contains the description of the table (is also used to insert/update records), methods are marked as [Transaction(TransactionOption.OrJoin)]

When testing my code with two simple migrations (first one create 2 tables, second one create a table and alter the two first) I've encourted the following issue in this part of code :

                foreach (var migrationToApply in migrationsToApply)
                {
                    var allDataSources1 = await _sqlDataSourcesGrain.GetDataSourcesAsync();

                    var sqlCommandOfMigration = await BuildMigrationAsync(migrationToApply, databaseMigrationWay);

                    var allDataSources2 = await _sqlDataSourcesGrain.GetDataSourcesAsync();

                    sqlCommands.AddRange(sqlCommandOfMigration);
                }

At the end of the first migration, the allDataSources2 contains 2 entries as expected, but then when entering the second loop the allDataSources1 contains 0 entries. In my mind it should contains the 2 entries from the first migration no?

Another question : while reading some posts trying to figure out what was happening I've read that the max execution time of an Orleans transaction is 8 secondes. Even it could be changed we think that is not a good idea. Because this limit can easily be reached by the time spent executing the sql code we tough of another solution : Before starting to alter ISqlDataSourcesGrain's state, make a copy of it. and then if the SQL isn't successfull restore this old state.
